extracted_json.title: Company Analysis

blocks.text: How do you analyze a technology company?
[Comment: In general Company Analysis precedes Industry Analysis]

items.text: 1.1 Vision statement: a high-level vision or idea about the future
Example: Microsoft (at its inception ≈ 1985) Vision: "A world with computers in every home, and at every desktop (in an office)"
1.2 Mission statement: a more specific view (or statement) of how the company might contribute to its vision.

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Framework for analyzing a technology company: vision and mission statements.
extracted_json.summary_gist: Introduces company analysis for technology firms, noting the relationship between industry (e.g., microprocessor industry) and company (e.g., Intel) analysis. States that all successful companies have a vision statement (a high-level idea about the future, e.g., Microsoft's 1985 vision of computers in every home and desktop) and a mission statement (a more specific view of how the company contributes to its vision).

transcription:
1.    Company   Analysis

      How  do  you  analyze  a  technology  company?

[figure]

[Comment :  In general   Company  Analysis  precedes
            Industry  Analysis ]

      All  successful  companies  have

1.1  Vision statement : a high-level vision or
                        idea about the future

      Example :  Microsoft (at its inception ≈ 1985)
                 Vision : "A world with computers
                 in every home, and at every desktop
                 (in an office)"

1.2  Mission statement : a more specific view
                         (or statement) of how the
                         company might contribute
                         to its vision.
